conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

Before I can assess your eligibility, I need to gather some information about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official guidance on Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children you'd like to claim Child Benefit for?** If yes, how many children?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to claim Child Benefit. I have two children - Alex, who is 14, and Blake, who is 19.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I can see you have two children - Alex (14) and Blake (19).

Let me ask about Blake first, as they are 19 years old. **Is Blake currently in approved education or training?** 

(For example, this could include A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs up to level 3, or other approved courses.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Blake is in approved education. He's doing his A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Since Blake is 19 and in approved education (A-levels), he may still qualify.

Now I need to ask a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your living arrangements with the children.

**Do both Alex and Blake live with you at home?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, actually only Blake lives with me at home. Alex doesn't live with me - they live with their other parent.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Since Alex doesn't live with you, I need to ask:

**Do you contribute financially towards Alex's upkeep?** For example, do you pay for things like food, clothes, pocket money, or contribute to their accommodation - and if so, is this at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment (which is currently around £25 per week for a child who is not the eldest)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially towards Alex's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I have a couple more questions:

**Has anyone else (such as Alex's other parent) already claimed Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, their other parent already claims Child Benefit for Alex since they live with them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you, that's very clear. Just a couple final questions to complete my assessment:

**Has Blake started any paid work for 24 hours or more per week, or started claiming any benefits in their own right** (such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake hasn't started working 24 hours or more a week, and they're not claiming any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary of Your Child Benefit Eligibility

**For Alex (age 14): Not Eligible ❌**

You are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex because:
- Alex lives with their other parent, not with you
- You are not contributing financially towards Alex's upkeep
- Alex's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for them
- Only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child

To be eligible when a child doesn't live with you, you would need to contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment towards their upkeep (things like food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation costs).

---

**For Blake (age 19): Eligible ✅**

You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake because:
- Blake lives with you in the UK
- Blake is under 20 years old and is in approved education (A-levels)
- Blake has not started working 24+ hours per week
- Blake is not claiming benefits in their own right

You can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Blake. The payment will continue as long as Blake remains in approved education or training and doesn't turn 20.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about your Child Benefit eligibility?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 14 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 14 (under 16)
  - Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 14 years old and lives with their other parent, not with you. You are not contributing financially towards Alex's upkeep. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child - either by living with them OR by paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after them. Since you don't live with Alex and are not making financial contributions to their upkeep, you are not considered responsible for Alex. Additionally, Alex's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for them, and only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 19 years old and is currently in approved education (doing A-levels at sixth form college). According to the eligibility rules, you normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Blake lives with you in the UK (Manchester), which means you are responsible for them. Blake has not started paid work for 24 hours or more per week and is not claiming benefits in their own right. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children: Alex (14) and Blake (19). The claimant lives in the UK. For Alex, the claimant is not eligible because Alex lives with their other parent who already claims Child Benefit for them, and the claimant does not contribute financially to Alex's upkeep. For Blake, the claimant is eligible because Blake lives with them, is under 20, is in approved education (A-levels), and has not started working 24+ hours per week or claiming benefits independently.

Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Blake.
